title: pseudotephrocactus vestitus
side_effects: Pseudotephrocactus vestitus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Improper handling or ingestion of this plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as inflammation and redness of the skin. In severe cases, it can cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. Additionally, the sap of the Pseudotephrocactus vestitus plant can cause contact dermatitis, a skin condition characterized by redness, itching, and blistering.
